heading: Support services for House during emergency; memorandum of understanding with an executive agency
label: 5546 Support services for House during emergency; memorandum of understanding with an executive agency
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 130h of this titleSection is from the Emergency Supplemental Act, 2002, which is div. B of the Department of Defense and Emergency Supplemental Appropriations for Recovery from and Response to Terrorist Attacks on the United States Act, 2002.

children.text: subject to subsection (b), the Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives and the head of an executive agency (as defined in ) may enter into a memorandum of understanding under which the agency may provide facilities, equipment, supplies, personnel, and other support services for the use of the House of Representatives during an emergency situation; andsection 105 of title 5
the Chief Administrative Officer and the head of the agency may take any action necessary to carry out the terms of the memorandum of understanding.

children.heading: Approval of Speaker required
children.label: b Approval of Speaker required
children.text: The Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives may not enter into a memorandum of understanding described in subsection (a)(1) without the approval of the Speaker of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: c Applicability
children.text: This section shall apply with respect to fiscal year 2002 and each succeeding fiscal year.
